statement: The objective function in AUTO HALLUSION maximizes the distance between the LVLM's generated text answer and the ground truth to produce hallucination, while a constraint ensures that the answer is dominated by the language prior rather than the visual input by controlling discrepancies within a tolerance ϵ.

statement: The paper introduces two image manipulation strategies for hallucination attacks on LVLMs: (1) correlated object removal, which removes an object with the strongest correlation to the scene context as determined by the minimum sum of language prior distances across contextual elements, and (2) abnormal object insertion, which inserts an object maximally unrelated to the context as determined by the maximum sum of language prior distances.

This is central to understanding how the attack works and is not common knowledge.

The abnormal object insertion strategy tries to in- sert an object not related to the existing contextual elements   into   the   scene   image   I s . ... The query question  q ∗ , which is also the abnor- mal object for insertion, should have the  maximum sum of distances  between its language prior and the ground truth information across all contextual elements in  C .
